channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Every Stripe decline code, what it actually means, and whether retrying will help
item.description: When a card payment fails in Stripe, you get a short string back — insufficient_funds, do_not_honor, authentication_required. Most teams glance at it and let Smart Retries hammer the card on a...

item.title: OAuth2 Login with JWT and Refresh Tokens in Spring Boot — The Setup You'll Rebuild Every Time
item.description: Most Spring Boot applications eventually need authentication. And many teams rebuild the same foundation every time. Add a login endpoint. Generate a token. Protect some APIs. Then production...

item.title: AMLA Is Coming Online: What the EU's New Anti–Money Laundering Authority Means for Builders and Regulators
item.description: If you work anywhere near payments, banking, crypto, or fintech in Europe, a new acronym is about to land in your backlog: AMLA — the EU's Authority for Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the...

item.title: Zero Trust for Home Labs: Bridging the Gap Between Enterprise and Enthusiast
item.description: The Evolution of the Home Lab: Why Enterprise Security Standards Matter For the modern cybersecurity professional, the home lab has transcended its origins as a mere hobbyist’s playground. Today,...

item.title: I Was Shocked by How Cheap LLMs Can Be — A Bootcamp Grad's Guide
item.description: I Was Shocked by How Cheap LLMs Can Be — A Bootcamp Grad's Guide Three months ago I graduated from a full-stack bootcamp. I was riding the high of building my first CRUD app when my mentor dropped...

item.title: Day 16: ClickHouse Dictionaries – Eliminating Expensive JOINs with High-Speed In-Memory Lookups
item.description: When working with analytical databases, enriching fact tables with customer information, product metadata, geographical mappings, or business attributes is a common requirement. Traditionally,...

item.title: PostgreSQL 2200F Error: Causes and Solutions Complete Guide
item.description: PostgreSQL Error 2200F: Zero Length Character String PostgreSQL error 2200F: zero_length_character_string occurs when an empty string ('') is passed to a function or operator that requires at...

item.title: Flowork Agent: A Self-Hosted AI Agent OS With Memory, Security, and Offline Capability
item.description: Most AI forgets you the moment you close the tab. A Flowork agent is something you own. It lives in a folder on your machine, carries its own memory, learns from its own mistakes, and keeps...

item.title:
Demo: https://codei-sync-t72r.onrender.com/
I built a real-time collaborative coding platform and would love some honest feedback.
The goal was to remove the friction that often comes with pair programming and collaborative coding sessions. Instead of in
item.description: codei-sync-t72r.onrender.com

item.title: I built a free, open-source AI calorie tracker because the others got greedy
item.description: Most calorie trackers have drifted into the same place: a subscription wall, ads, a mandatory account, and your eating data quietly monetized. I wanted the opposite, so I built Fud AI — a free,...

item.title: Never Post’s Mike Rugnetta on the creative process and the value of reliable power
item.description: Mike Rugnetta is a writer, podcast host, producer, audio engineer, educator, musician, sound designer, and father. In short, the man wears a lot of hats. He's the cocreator and host of the...

item.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
item.description: Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution. The...

item.title: My yard is dying, so I made an app for that
item.description: When I returned to my computer five minutes after giving Gemini a lengthy prompt, I had two things: a functional app in a preview window, and a message about a bug. "~ Channel is unrecoverably...

item.title: Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
item.description: On Friday evening, the government ordered Anthropic to block access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nations, both inside and outside the US, due to national security concerns. That order...

item.title: Echo Isle is a pint-sized adventure inspired by classic Zelda
item.description: Echo Isle is heavily inspired by The Legend of Zelda, and it's not afraid to show it: The retro graphics bear a striking resemblance to Link's Awakening, the main character wears a blue tunic and...

item.title: Apple’s new AI photo editing tools mostly work, for better and worse
item.description: The most popular camera in the world just got its first set of serious AI photo editing features, and I don't think any of us are ready. As far as AI photo editing goes, the new features in iOS 27...

item.title: A better way to manage all your screenshots
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 132, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, happy soccer, and also you can read all the old editions at the...

item.title: The future of Hollywood isn’t feeding prompts into vanilla gen AI models
item.description: For all the noise that's been made about how generative AI is poised to revolutionize the filmmaking industry, there haven't really been any projects created with the technology that felt like the...

item.title: A Court Has Ruled That Google Is Liable for False Statements Generated by AI Overviews
item.description: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.

item.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to...

item.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living
item.description: Andrew Yang made a list of everything Americans overpay for — housing, food, wireless — and thinks the next startup gold rush is giving that money back.

item.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
item.description: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people,"...

item.title: Anthropic Says It’s Taking Claude Fable 5 Offline to Comply With US Government Order
item.description: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.

item.title: Valve just imported 13 tons of VR headsets in one day
item.description: On June 10th, the German container ship Posen docked in Los Angeles after a two-week voyage from Shanghai. As Valve watcher Brad Lynch notes, it was almost certainly carrying the first mass...

item.title: Meta Employees Absolutely Hate Mark Zuckerberg’s Plan for a Companywide AI Hackathon
item.description: “I’m not sure that this company supports a hackathon culture anymore,” one employee posted in a forum open to the entire staff.

item.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and...

item.title: Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it
item.description: A new report suggests the unit, which employs 6,500 people, is on the verge of revolt.

item.title: ‘Tell Him He’s a Piece of Shit’: Meta’s New AI Unit Is a Total Mess
item.description: Executives and employees alike are struggling with Meta’s chaotic AI strategy, according to sources and internal discussions reviewed by WIRED.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid-Inspired Fluid Pump
item.description: This fluid pump was inspired by the way squids propel themselves through the water. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t...

item.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
item.description: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.
